stmt: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)], ∀ x y : α, ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ x → ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ y → ((x ≤ (⊥ : α)) ↔ (y ≤ (⊥ : α)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∀ x : α, ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ x → ((g.1 ≤ (⊥ : α)) ↔ (x ≤ (⊥ : α)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] (f : α → α), (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → f y ≤ f x) → ∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → (ACR.Equivalent x (f x) ↔ ACR.Equivalent y (f y))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α} {x : α}, ACR.Equivalent x g.1 → ACR.Equivalent x (ACR.Reft.reft x)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], (∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)) → (ACR.Inconsistent α ↔ ∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊥ : α))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], (∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)) → (¬ ACR.Consistent α ↔ ∀ x : α, ¬¬ (x ≤ (⊥ : α)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], (∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)) → (¬ ACR.Consistent α ↔ ∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊥ : α))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ¬ ACR.Consistent α ↔ ∀ x : α, ⊠x ≤ (⊥ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ (⊤ : α)) → ∀ x : α, (x ≤ ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ↔ x ≤ (⊤ : α))
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ¬ (ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ (⊤ : α))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent x (⊤ : α) ↔ (⊤ : α) ≤ x
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ⊠(⊥ : α) = (⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, □(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ □(⊠x)

There exists a three-element chain α = {0 < 1 < 2} with ⊥ = 0, ⊤ = 2, provability operator defined by □x = x, and refutability operator defined by ⊠0 = 2, ⊠1 = 1, and ⊠2 = 1, such that α satisfies the ACR, Prov, Reft, APS, and C5 axioms, has a Gödel fixpoint, and for x = 0 one has ¬ ACR.Equivalent (□(⊠x)) (□(⊠(⊤ : α))).
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent (□(⊠x)) (□(⊠(⊤ : α)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent (⊠x) (⊠(⊠x))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent (⊠(⊠x)) g.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ x → ∀ y : α, ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ y → ACR.Equivalent (⊠x) (⊠y)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ x → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent (□(⊠x)) (□g.1)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ⊠(⊠x) ≤ ⊠x
There exists a finite preorder model carrying ACR, Prov, Reft, APS, and C5 structure, together with a Gödel fixpoint and an element x, such that ACR.Equivalent (⊠x) (⊠(⊠x)) fails.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent (⊠x) (⊠⊠x)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∃ g h : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g ≠ h) → ∃ x y : α, x ≤ y ∧ y ≤ x ∧ x ≠ y
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → ∀ x y : α, ACR.Equivalent x (⊠(⊤ : α)) → ACR.Equivalent y (⊠(⊤ : α)) → x = y

There exists a structure on `Bool` with preorder relation `x ≤ y :↔ True`, top element `⊤ = true`, bottom element `⊥ = false`, provability operator `□x = x`, and refutability operator `⊠x = x`, such that `Bool` satisfies the `ACR`, `APS`, and `C5` axioms, and `Bool` also carries some `PartialOrder` instance.

For `Bool` equipped with the relation `x ≤ y :↔ True`, top element `⊤ = true`, bottom element `⊥ = false`, provability operator `□x = x`, and refutability operator `⊠x = x`, both `false` and `true` satisfy `x ≡ ⊠x`, and `false ≠ true`.
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : PartialOrder α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∃ g h : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g ≠ h

For every type α with instances [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], if the order relation of the `PartialOrder α` instance coincides with the preorder carried by `ACR α`, then every two Gödel fixpoints `g h : ACR.GödelFixpoint α` are equal.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ACR.Equivalent (□(⊠(⊤ : α))) (⊠(⊤ : α))
There exists a finite type α with instances `ACR α`, `ACR.Prov α`, `ACR.Reft α`, `ACR.APS α`, and `ACR.C5 α` such that `¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent x (⊠x) → ACR.Equivalent x (⊠(⊤ : α))

∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ∃ x : α, ACR.Equivalent x (⊠(⊤ : α)) ∧ ¬ ACR.Equivalent x (⊠x)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] (f : α → α), (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → f y ≤ f x) → ∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ACR.Equivalent (f x) (f y)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] {x y z : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ((z ≤ x ↔ z ≤ y) ∧ (x ≤ z ↔ y ≤ z))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ x : α, ∀ g h : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent x g.1 ↔ ACR.Equivalent x h.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ACR.Equivalent (ACR.Reft.reft (⊤ : α)) (ACR.Reft.reft (ACR.Reft.reft (⊤ : α)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → x = y) → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → □(⊠(⊤ : α)) = ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → x = y) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □g.1 = g.1
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : PartialOrder α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∃ x y : α, ACR.Equivalent x y ∧ x ≠ y

There exists a type α with instances [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] and two distinct Gödel fixpoints g h : ACR.GödelFixpoint α such that ACR.Equivalent g.1 h.1.

For every type α with instances [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], if the order relation of the `PartialOrder α` instance coincides with the preorder carried by `ACR α`, then every Gödel fixpoint `g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α` satisfies `g.1 = ⊠(⊤ : α)`.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → ∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → x = y
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 = ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], ACR.Consistent α → ((⊤ : α) ≠ ⊥)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], ACR.Consistent α → Nontrivial α

For every type `α` and every `t b x : α`, if `t ≠ b`, `t ≠ x`, and `x ≠ b`, then there exists an `ACR α` structure whose order relation is equality, whose top element is `t`, and whose bottom element is `b`, such that `ACR.Consistent α` and `¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ x) ∧ ¬ (x ≤ (⊥ : α))` both hold.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], ACR.Consistent α → (∃ x : α, ¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ x) ∧ ¬ (x ≤ (⊥ : α))) → ∃ a b c : α, a ≠ b ∧ a ≠ c ∧ b ≠ c
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ x : α, ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ x → (ACR.Consistent α ↔ ¬ (x ≤ (⊥ : α)))
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (x : α), ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ x ∧ ¬ (x ≤ (⊥ : α)) ∧ ¬ ACR.Consistent α

For every type α with instances [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], if the order relation of the `PartialOrder α` instance coincides with the preorder carried by `ACR α`, then `(⊠(⊥ : α) ≤ (⊤ : α))` if and only if `⊠(⊥ : α) = (⊤ : α)`.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ (⊤ : α)) → ((ACR.Reft.reft (⊥ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α)) ↔ ACR.Inconsistent α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.HenkinFixpoint α
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ x : α, x ≤ ACR.Prov.prov x → ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Prov.prov x) ≤ ACR.Prov.prov x → ∃ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 = ACR.Prov.prov x
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ x : α, ACR.Equivalent x (ACR.Prov.prov x) → ACR.Equivalent (ACR.Prov.prov x) (ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Prov.prov x))

∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∀ x : α, ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Prov.prov x) ≤ ACR.Prov.prov x) → ∀ y : α, ACR.Equivalent (ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Reft.reft y)) (ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Reft.reft y)))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], ((⊤ : α) = (⊥ : α)) → ACR.Inconsistent α

For any `hA : ACR Int` and `hR : @ACR.Reft Int hA`, if the preorder on `Int` induced by `hA` is the usual integer order and `@ACR.Reft.reft Int hA hR x = 0` for every `x : Int`, then `Nonempty (@ACR.GödelFixpoint Int hA hR)`.
∀ (hA : ACR Int) (hR : @ACR.Reft Int hA), (∀ x y : Int, @LE.le Int hA.toLE x y ↔ x ≤ y) → (∀ x : Int, @ACR.Reft.reft Int hA hR x = 0) → ∀ g : @ACR.GödelFixpoint Int hA hR, g.1 = 0
∀ (hA : ACR Int) (hR : @ACR.Reft Int hA), (∀ x y : Int, @LE.le Int hA.toLE x y ↔ x ≤ y) → (∀ x : Int, @ACR.Reft.reft Int hA hR x = 0) → ∀ g h : @ACR.GödelFixpoint Int hA hR, g = h
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ □x ∧ □x ≤ x
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ x : α, ¬ (□x ≤ x)) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
∀ (hA : ACR Int) (hP : @ACR.Prov Int hA) (hR : @ACR.Reft Int hA) (hAPS : @ACR.APS Int hA hP hR), (∀ x y : Int, @LE.le Int hA.toLE x y ↔ x ≤ y) → ¬ Nonempty (@ACR.C5 Int hA hP hR hAPS)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∀ t : α, ∃ x : α, ¬ x ≤ t) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.C5 α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α], ∀ c : α, (⊤ : α) = c → (⊥ : α) = c → (∀ x : α, ⊠x = c) → (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → □x ≤ □y) → c ≤ □c → Nonempty (ACR.APS α)

There exist `hA : ACR Int`, `hP : @ACR.Prov Int hA`, `hR : @ACR.Reft Int hA`, and `hAPS : @ACR.APS Int hA hP hR` such that the induced preorder on `Int` is the usual order, `@Top.top Int hA.toTop = 0`, `@Bot.bot Int hA.toBot = 0`, `@ACR.Prov.prov Int hA hP x = x + 1` for every `x : Int`, `@ACR.Reft.reft Int hA hR x = 0` for every `x : Int`, and `¬ ACR.Equivalent (ACR.Prov.prov (ACR.Reft.reft (@Top.top Int hA.toTop))) (ACR.Reft.reft (@Top.top Int hA.toTop))`.

∀ (k : Int) (hA : ACR Int) (hP : @ACR.Prov Int hA), (∀ x y : Int, @LE.le Int hA.toLE x y ↔ x ≤ y) → (∀ x : Int, @ACR.Prov.prov Int hA hP x = x + k) → (Nonempty (@ACR.HenkinFixpoint Int hA hP) ↔ k = 0)
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ∃ h₁ h₂ : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, ¬ ACR.Equivalent h₁.1 h₂.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → □y ≤ x → x ≡ □y) → (∀ h₁ h₂ : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h₁.1 ≤ h₂.1) → ∀ h₁ h₂ : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h₁.1 ≡ h₂.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [PartialOrder α] {h : α}, ACR.Equivalent h (ACR.Prov.prov h) ↔ h = ACR.Prov.prov h
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [PartialOrder α], Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ h : α, h = □h
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ∀ n : ℕ, ACR.Equivalent ((ACR.Prov.prov^[n]) x) ((ACR.Prov.prov^[n]) y)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α], ∀ {x y z : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ACR.Equivalent y z → ACR.Equivalent x z

For every type α with instances [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], every Henkin fixpoint h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, and every natural number n with 1 ≤ n, the n-fold iterate of □ applied to h.1 is equivalent to h.1.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≤ □(g.1)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, (□(□(g.1)) ≤ □(g.1)) ↔ ∃ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 = □(g.1)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □(□g.1) ≤ □g.1
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (□(□g.1) ≤ □g.1)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □g.1 ≤ g.1
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ (□(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α))
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ¬ (□g.1 ≤ g.1)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → □(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Equivalent (⊤ : α) (□(⊤ : α))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent h.1 (⊤ : α)

∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ((ACR.Equivalent x (ACR.Prov.prov x) ↔ ACR.Equivalent y (ACR.Prov.prov y)) ∧ (ACR.Equivalent x (ACR.Reft.reft x) ↔ ACR.Equivalent y (ACR.Reft.reft y)))
∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (x y : α), ACR.Equivalent (ACR.Prov.prov x) (ACR.Prov.prov y) ∧ ¬ ACR.Equivalent x y
For every type α with instance [ACR α], the quotient of α by the relation ACR.Equivalent carries a partial order defined by [x] ≤ [y] if and only if x ≤ y.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] (f : α → α), (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → f x ≤ f y) → ∀ {x y : α}, ACR.Equivalent x y → ACR.Equivalent (f x) (f y)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x = □x)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → ∀ {x : α}, ACR.Equivalent x (□x) ↔ x = □x

For every type α with instances [ACR α] [PartialOrder α] [ACR.Reft α], if the order relation of the `PartialOrder α` instance coincides with the preorder carried by `ACR α`, then for every `x : α`, `x ≡ ⊠x` if and only if `x = ⊠x`.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ⊠g.1 ≡ ⊠(⊠(⊤ : α))
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □g.1 ≤ ⊠g.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, □(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ g.1
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ACR.Equivalent g.1 (□g.1)
